model.label: MiMo-V2-Pro
model.description: MiMo • The flagship foundation for the agentic era, built for complex, long-horizon, multi-step agentic tasks.

pick.rationale: Virginia holds a slight analytical edge with superior KenPom (13 vs 15) and Torvik (14 vs 15) rankings, featuring elite defensive efficiency (rank 14) that matches Tennessee's strength. The Cavaliers' 29-4 record and ACC tournament championship momentum provide a psychological edge, while Tennessee's 22-11 record and recent inconsistency (2-3 in last five games) raise concerns. Although both teams have similar defensive profiles, Virginia's superior record and tournament experience under Tony Bennett give them a narrow advantage in this closely contested matchup.

reasoningStep.title: Tennessee vs Virginia Round of 32 Analysis
reasoningStep.summary: Both teams are defensively elite with nearly identical analytical profiles, but Virginia's superior record and recent form give them a slight edge.
reasoningStep.evidence: KenPom rankings: Virginia 13, Tennessee 15. Torvik rankings: Virginia 14, Tennessee 15. Defensive efficiency ranks: Virginia 14, Tennessee 13. Records: Virginia 29-4, Tennessee 22-11. Recent form: Virginia 4-1 in last five games, Tennessee 2-3. Both teams have strong defensive identities, but Virginia's consistency and ACC tournament success provide a marginal advantage.

The game will take place on Friday in Philadelphia in what will likely prove to be a very tough draw for the Vols. Despite being a mid-major, Miami rebounds hard and crashes the glass on defense. They run a free-flowing offense under Kevin Steele that doesn't run set plays, but rather a continuous motion offense. They are No. 6 in the country in effective field goal percentage on offense.
